profile_bio.description: Codex App @OpenAI

quoted_tweet.text:
We added a device tool bar to the Codex in-app browser, so it's easier to build and test responsive apps! 

Now, you can have Codex test your app in different dimensions, so it can fix bugs & improve UI for every device.

Just click the 3 dots on the right of the URL bar to use it!

As part of this change, we also:
- improved the speed of browser use (~30% in my vibe testing)
- tightened the animation of the cursor 
- added a way to hide the composer in full screen mode
- fixed a few bugs for browser use in Windows 

@olivertepman and @jnpdx cooked on this one! 

Try it out and let us know what you think!

kept.text:
NEWS: Birchall’s testimony winds down on day 4 of Elon Musk vs OpenAI trial.

OpenAI’s attorney Bradley Wilson concluded his questioning. A lawyer representing Microsoft briefly stood up to ask Birchall a few questions, with that exchange lasting less than five minutes.

Elon Musk’s lawyer Robert Kry is now carrying out his redirect. With court only in session for 20 more minutes, Birchall’s testimony could conclude the day.

profile_bio.description:
#1 News Brand on X | Free Speech 

X | Tesla | SpaceX | Starlink |  xAI | Neuralink | Musk Economy

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Jared Birchall takes the stand on day 4, faces questions on Elon Musk’s OpenAI donations and acquisition bid.

Elon Musk’s lawyer Robert Kry walked Birchall through various financial documents and specific donations Elon Musk made to OpenAI. Birchall’s answers were short and concise, with questioning moving quickly.

Kry asked Birchall to explain his understanding of why a Elon Musk-led group of investors made a multi-billion-dollar bid to acquire OpenAI last year. Birchall testified the bid was meant to set a market value for OpenAI’s assets and prevent the diminishment of those holdings.

OpenAI attorney Bradley R. Wilson then took over questioning, asking Birchall about his many roles across Elon Musk’s companies. Wilson also asked about funds Elon Musk put into “DAFs,” or donor-advised funds, managed by Vanguard and Fidelity.

Wilson asked if Birchall knew whether Elon Musk had any legal right to direct where the funds should go once contributed to a DAF. Birchall said he was not a lawyer and did not know precisely.

The exchange suggested OpenAI leaders believed they were free to use the funds Elon Musk donated however they wished, and that their use was not formally restricted by Elon Musk or his offices.

profile_bio.description:
#1 News Brand on X | Free Speech

kept.text:
NEWS: Jared Birchall takes the stand on day 4, faces questions on Elon Musk’s OpenAI donations and acquisition bid.

Elon Musk’s lawyer Robert Kry walked Birchall through various financial documents and specific donations Elon Musk made to OpenAI. Birchall’s answers were short and concise, with questioning moving quickly.

Kry asked Birchall to explain his understanding of why a Elon Musk-led group of investors made a multi-billion-dollar bid to acquire OpenAI last year. Birchall testified the bid was meant to set a market value for OpenAI’s assets and prevent the diminishment of those holdings.

OpenAI attorney Bradley R. Wilson then took over questioning, asking Birchall about his many roles across Elon Musk’s companies. Wilson also asked about funds Elon Musk put into “DAFs,” or donor-advised funds, managed by Vanguard and Fidelity.

Wilson asked if Birchall knew whether Elon Musk had any legal right to direct where the funds should go once contributed to a DAF. Birchall said he was not a lawyer and did not know precisely.

The exchange suggested OpenAI leaders believed they were free to use the funds Elon Musk donated however they wished, and that their use was not formally restricted by Elon Musk or his offices.

profile_bio.description:

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Judge reprimands OpenAI lawyer and reminds Elon Musk he's not a lawyer on day 4 of trial.

At the end of William Savitt's cross-examination, Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers reprimanded the attorney, telling him he must allow Elon Musk to complete his answers without interrupting.

Elon Musk had tried to paint Savitt as asking improper "leading" questions and "leading answers."

The judge then turned to Elon Musk and informed the jury that he is not a lawyer and not an expert on evidence or matters concerning allowable questions and exhibits. She said Elon Musk has "not taken a class in evidence."

Elon Musk shot back that he has "technically" taken "law 101," drawing laughter in the courtroom.

Savitt apologized to both Elon Musk and the judge, saying he was concerned about getting his final questions in within his limited time.

kept.text:
おそらくイーロン嫌いな人がイーロン対OpenAI裁判でイーロンがいかに負けそうか記事を書く。それによるとイーロンは「OpenAIは非営利企業を盗んだ！」って主張したけど「具体的には何の法律に触れたの？」と詰められるとしどろもどろ。そしてOpenAI側の弁護士がナルホド君ばりにイーロンの証言の矛盾点に「異議あり」しまくってしまう。例えばイーロンが「テスラはAGIなんて目指してないよ。自動運転作ってるだけ」と言ったら弁護士が「待った！あなたは以前にこうツイートしてる。”テスラは史上初のAGIを作った企業になるだろう”…と！」グサッ！！イーロンが言ってる事全体は間違ってないのにあまりにもイーロンが弁護士から論破され過ぎていると記事は言う

kept.text:
我们来换一个角度看看，为啥 AI  时代只有中美两个国家这个事：

💡美国在造：Microsoft+OpenAI Stargate 的百万卡计划、Google TPU 超大规模集群、Meta+Amazon 海量GPU数据中心 

💡中国在造：华为 Ascend 万卡集群、阿里云+百度+腾讯自建国产算力工厂、字节/DeepSeek 等超大训练基地。

其他的欧洲、日本、韩国等190多个国家……他们在干啥？ 

他们在讨论‘绿色能源’和‘监管政策’。 

他们压根就没有自建万卡能力、没有匹配的电力基础设施、没有本土供应链，基本全靠进口中美 GPU/芯片。

未来 AI 训练成本 90% 是电费和算力，他们设甚至连自己的民用电民用能源都不够用。”

kept.text:
NEWS: Judge considers motion to strike some of Birchall’s testimony on day 4 of trial.

After the jury left, Judge Gonzalez Rogers heard arguments over a motion to strike portions of Jared Birchall’s testimony about xAI’s bid to acquire OpenAI. 

Elon Musk’s lawyers sought to eliminate at least part of it, plus possibly documents unearthed in discovery, from the jury’s consideration.

OpenAI attorney Bradley Wilson pressed Birchall to clarify how he became convinced Sam Altman was inappropriately negotiating “on both sides of the table” to restructure OpenAI.

Birchall said some of his information came from public sources like news, with other details from lawyers. He testified he had no inside information from bankers, OpenAI leaders or board members about what was happening at the company.

Gonzalez Rogers asked Birchall clarifying questions and said she would decide how to handle his testimony in the coming days.

profile_bio.description:
#1 News Brand on X | Free Speech 

X | Tesla | SpaceX | Starlink |  xAI | Neuralink | Musk Economy

quoted_tweet.text:
NEWS: Jury leaves for the day on day 4 of Elon Musk vs OpenAI trial as judge meets with lawyers.

Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers let the jury go a few minutes early to confer with attorneys about an objection. There are no proceedings tomorrow, so the jury won’t return until Monday morning.

Gonzalez Rogers gave jurors strict instructions not to discuss the case or conduct any research about it over the next three days. Lawyers for Elon Musk and OpenAI are now awaiting further instructions.

kept.text:
OpenAI's promise of "open" AI was the biggest con in tech history and Elon Musk is the only one fighting to take the mask off

Sam Altman and his crew looted the nonprofit....quietly turning it into a closed, for-profit empire

This was Judas-level betrayal

恐ろしい...本当に

イーロン・マスク氏は、OpenAIの内部告発者の「自殺」について、アルトマンに疑惑の目を向けている。

内部告発者のスチル・バラジはOpenAIの元研究者（26歳）
・在職中にOpenAIの著作権侵害を告発して退社
・2024年11月、サンフランシスコの自室で死亡した。

しかし、部屋では
・防犯カメラの配線が切断されていた
・2つの部屋に血痕があった
・別人の「かつら」が転がっていた
・死亡直前にバラジはフードデリバリーを注文していた

タッカー・カールソンはアルトマンにインタビューで「彼は自殺したと思うか？」と問い詰めた。

アルトマンは、この事件についてできる限り多くの情報を調べたが「私には自殺に見える」と答えた。

マスクはこジョー・ローガンとのインタビュー（動画）で、このアルトマンの様子を見て「有罪かどうかは分からないが、これ以上疑わしく見えることはない」と述べた。

さらに、「この件は隠蔽しようとするのではなく、徹底的な調査を求めるべきだ」と指摘した。

マスクも動画の中で言ってるが、なんで人のカツラがあるの？
なんで防犯カメラが切断されてるの？
自殺する人がデリバリー頼むか？
刑事コロンボなら解決してくれると思うのだが。。。

profile_bio.description: 世界で起こる様々な情報をお届けします

quoted_tweet.text:
マスク氏が勝つ可能性大。そうなれば、OpenAIは解体される。

・法廷でアルトマンの「証拠」が自爆

OpenAIはマスクを追い詰めるため、あるテキストを証拠提出。そこにはアルトマン自身の言葉があった

「私は個人的に株（エクイティ）を持っていないし、持ったこともない」

しかしそのテキストを書いた時点で、アルトマンはすでにOpenAI Startupファンドの経済的権益を保有していた。

マスク氏はアルトマンは詐欺師だと言っている。

自ら提出した証拠が、自らの虚偽申告の証明になった。
これが今、法廷を揺るがしている。
ーーーーーー
動画は過去にアルトマンがケネディ議員に「株式」は一切持っていないと言った場面。

その後、アルトマンは株式を所有していたことが明らかになった。

ケネディ議員「なるほど。かなり稼いでいるのか？」

アルトマン「いいえ、そうではありません。医療保険が賄える程度の報酬です。OpenAIの持ち分（株式）は一切持っていません。」

ケネディ議員「本当か？それは興味深いな。弁護士かエージェントをつけた方がいい。」

アルトマン「え、何が必要だと？」

ケネディ議員「弁護士かエージェントだ。」

kept.text:
Elon Musk: OpenAI is like a non-profit that should preserve the Amazon rainforest, but instead they chop down trees and sell the wood.

“I came up with the name OpenAI as open source and as a nonprofit and I funded OpenAI for the first roughly $50 million. And it was intended to be a nonprofit open-source company. And now, they're trying to change that for their own financial benefit into a for-profit company that is closed source. So this would be like, let's say you funded a non-profit to help preserve the Amazon rainforest, but instead of doing that, they became a lumber company, chopped down the forest and sold the wood. You'd be like, wait a second, that's not what I funded. That's OpenAI.”

Interview at the Qatar Economic Forum, May 20, 2025

kept.text:
The $GOBLIN mindshare is not random.

It sits at the exact intersection of three things the internet loves right now:

AI weirdness, memes, and the race to AGI

For the last few days, AI Twitter has been obsessed with OpenAI’s “goblin problem.” Reports say GPT-5.5/Codex started overusing words like goblins, gremlins, trolls and other weird creatures, to the point OpenAI apparently had to add instructions telling the model not to mention them unless relevant.  

That is why the meme hits.

The lore is simple:

The labs tried to summon AGI.

Instead, the models started whispering about goblins.

AI researchers saw a weird alignment artefact.

The goblin is the perfect AI-era meme because it represents the part of LLMs we still do not fully control, the strange emergent personality hiding inside prediction engines, RLHF, agentic tools, system prompts and internet training data.

It is not “Artificial General Intelligence.”

It is “Artificial Goblin Intelligence.”

Popular AI accounts have already been joking about this, from posts about GPT-5.5 loving “goblin mode,” to charts tracking the rise of goblin/gremlin/troll language across model outputs, to people riffing on the now-famous “never talk about goblins…” prompt line.  

That is the mindshare.

$GOBLIN is not just another Solana memecoin trying to invent lore after launch.

The lore already exists.

OpenAI accidentally gave the internet a myth:

Inside every LLM is a tiny goblin trying to escape the system prompt.

kept.text:
it’s time to be honest. 

I admit publicly I am codex-pilled

here are THE 7 reasons why I believe codex is winning

1) raw power: @steipete cold started the arc demonstrating the superiority of codex that he used to build OpenClaw while wearing gymshark. GPT-5.5 is a beast

2) customer-first: @thsottiaux the great resetter of limits, giver of tokens, showed that there will be mistakes but OpenAI will always side with the customer first

3) user-experience: @ajambrosino is making the app so delightful. now all others (cursor, claude code desktop) are copying it

4) goofiness and fun: @sama finally admits that the G in agi stands for goblin 

5) generosity: feels like $20 in Codex is $100 in Claude Code and $150 in cursor. @romainhuet please keep it that way!

6) vision: the codex app is really becoming for anything. I haven’t used ChatGPT in months! @embirico is killing it 

7) features: the built-in worktrees, Image-2 within Codex. Computer and Browser use. /goal, so many plugins, automations…

Now you know what’s next: when is the iOS app please?!? @Dimillian ;)
